# REVIEW.md (jeremycowcher.com)

What to check before a jeremycowcher.com change ships. This is a static personal site built by a custom `node build.js` (marked + `@gemka/core`), with no framework and no Clerk/Supabase of its own, so the shared block's auth, API-surface, and database sections are mostly N/A here. The block between the shared-review markers is synced from `claude-kit/review.md` by `sync-review.mjs`; do not hand-edit it. Site-specific checks live below the markers.

<!-- shared-review:start (generated by sync-review; edit review.md) -->

## What REVIEW.md is

REVIEW.md is the review-time counterpart to CLAUDE.md. CLAUDE.md says how to build in this repo; REVIEW.md says what to check before a change ships. A reviewer (Jeremy, a Claude review subagent, or the `code-standards-audit` skill) reads it against a diff and confirms each item, so the checks live in the repo instead of one person's head.

The block between the shared-review markers is synced from `claude-kit/review.md` and applies to every repo. Checks specific to this repo live below the markers. When a shared check needs the mechanical grep-level pass, run the `code-standards-audit` skill: it is the executable half of this list (Rules 1 to 3 and the red flags below map to its steps).

## One change, verified (Dyson 5,127)

- [ ] The diff solves one problem. Independent changes are split, not batched, so a regression has one cause and not five.
- [ ] The change was verified the way its blast radius demands: data-touching changes (migrations, bulk edits, schema) rehearsed on scrimmage first; user-visible changes checked on staging/preseason. The repo tail says which environments apply.
- [ ] Nothing user-facing is going public without Jeremy's explicit, in-the-moment consent for that specific item. "Wrap this up" is not consent.

## External data: no silent fallbacks

- [ ] No `?? 0`, `?? ''`, `?? false`, `|| []` (or similar) papering over a missing field from Supabase rows, API responses, webhook payloads, or localStorage as it becomes typed state. A missing required field throws with the row id and field name; a genuinely optional field is typed optional, not defaulted. (code-standards-audit Rule 1. Check the field's nullability in the migration or with a null-count query before recommending a throw.)
- [ ] Every select that can grow is paginated. PostgREST silently caps a select at 1000 rows, so unbounded reads lose data with no error.
- [ ] An unauthenticated read is never treated as authoritative. A lapsed token degrades the request to anon, which RLS answers with an empty set and no error, so reads verify a live token before merging local state away.

## Server logging

- [ ] Server code (API routes, webhooks, server-imported lib modules, cron handlers, scripts) logs through Pino (`pino({ name: 'moduleName' })`), never `console.*`. Client and browser code is exempt. Any intentional server `console.*` carries a comment on the line above saying why. (code-standards-audit Rule 2.)

## API surface

- [ ] Every route under `src/app/api/` (or the repo equivalent) has one of: a client-side fetch caller, a Vercel cron entry in `vercel.json`, a documented external webhook registration, or a written justification comment. (code-standards-audit Rule 3.)
- [ ] RED FLAG: no public or unauthenticated endpoint reads from or writes to the database. (Reference: the McKinsey Lilli breach, March 2026, where 22 unauthenticated endpoints enabled a 2-hour SQL-injection breach.)
- [ ] Webhooks verify their signature (svix for Clerk) and are idempotent on redelivery.

## Auth and identity (Clerk / GemKa)

- [ ] Clerk secret keys stay server-only, and the environment has the right test-vs-live keys.
- [ ] Reverification-gated Clerk mutations (email change, passkey create/delete, and similar) go through `useReverification(...)`, not raw calls, or they return 403 `session_reverification_required`.
- [ ] No change to the GemKa cross-domain sign-up or auto-SSO flow without checking `ALLOWED_AFTER_SIGNUP_ORIGINS` and the origin-tag contract in `auth-redirects.ts`.
- [ ] clerk-js version assumptions still hold. The satellite return handshake depends on v5 behavior; a v6 bump silently returns users signed out unless sign-in is Clerk-initiated.

## Supabase: schema, RLS, backups

- [ ] A new table ships with RLS on and the intended policy (public-read vs auth-read vs service-role-only), and the service-role key never reaches the client.
- [ ] Any alteration or deletion (changing or dropping existing tables or columns, bulk updates, deleting rows) was discussed with Jeremy and rehearsed on scrimmage before prod. Pure adds are exempt.
- [ ] The change does not break the daily backup or the quarterly restore drill: no renamed table the dump or `backup_runs` reporting keys on, no new unbacked-up store of real user data.

## Secrets, abuse, rate limiting

- [ ] No secret, key, or token added to client-exposed code, a committed env file, or a log line.
- [ ] A new external-facing or expensive endpoint (discovery agent, email send, affiliate link, payment, auth) considers rate limiting and abuse protection, and a risky one sits behind a kill-switch flag where the repo supports flags.

## Email (Resend)

- [ ] Resend is constructed inside the handler, never at module scope. Module-scope init breaks the Vercel build because the key is absent at build time.

## Shared design system (@gemka/core)

- [ ] A style that belongs to `@gemka/core` (tokens.css / chrome.css, or a repo's generated `gk-tokens` / `gk-chrome` block) is changed in the package and re-synced, not forked locally. A change to shared chrome is flagged as cross-product.
- [ ] No `text-transform: uppercase` or ALL-CAPS copy introduced unprompted. It reads as AI slop to Jeremy. When fixing a style bug, change only the property that is wrong and match the sibling element pointed at.

## Prose in the diff

- [ ] No em dashes in any prose the change ships: UI copy, taglines, commit message, PR body, descriptions. Use commas, periods, parentheses, or a colon. Established "Title — Author" naming patterns are the one exempt case.

## Cross-product awareness

- [ ] If the change touches something that could generalize (rate limiting, Clerk/GemKa identity, Supabase RLS or backups, Resend, @gemka/core, the cross-sell flow, webhook/idempotency), it is flagged to Jeremy and checked against `claude-kit/cross-product-log.md`, adding a new entry if the pattern is new.

<!-- shared-review:end -->

## Repo-specific checks (jeremycowcher.com)

Checks below come from its CLAUDE.md ("Quotes and sourced content come from IdeaKache only", "Build pipeline", "Key patterns and conventions", "Non-obvious things that will bite you", "Deploy workflow").

- [ ] Publishing: a post goes live only by moving it out of `posts/_drafts/` into the live posts folder, and only with Jeremy's explicit in-the-moment consent for that specific post.
- [ ] Sourced content: every quote, attribution, or excerpt comes from IdeaKache (its Supabase `content` table filtered `verified = 'true'`, attributions via `authors` / `content_authors`), never the open web and never generated or paraphrased from memory. If a wanted quote is not in IdeaKache, the change stopped and asked Jeremy rather than substituting a web version, inventing one, or fixing attribution from memory.
- [ ] Build and deploy (static site): nothing needed was placed in `dist/` (it is gitignored and wiped via `rmSync` + `mkdirSync` on every build); per-page CSS inlining keeps `@gemka/core/tokens.css` before `style.css` (tokens must be defined first); work is on `dev`, production ships only by merge to `main` with a descriptive merge message (not the default); and post URLs stay `/posts/{slug}` with `vercel.json` keeping clean URLs and no trailing slashes.
- [ ] Content pipeline: every markdown post has complete frontmatter (`title`, `date` as `YYYY-MM-DD`, `description`), since the parser silently defaults a missing date to `2026-01-01`; any `.html` post carries a leading `<!--post-->` metadata comment before `<!DOCTYPE html>` and is emitted verbatim (never re-wrapped in the page template); and post sort stays date DESC then slug DESC (so Part II sorts above Part I on the same date).
- [ ] CSS regressions that must not return: the hero uses `100svh` not `100vh` (100vh hides the mobile Read button); every CSS variable used is defined in `:root` (a missing var fails silently); colors reference `--gk-*` tokens with no pure white / black / orange and accent oxblood `var(--gk-accent)` `#c8102e`; and Fraunces stays weight 500 for headings (the hero rotating quotes `.hero-subtitle` are the weight-360 exception), body stays Inter.
